chunk_summary: Phosphate binder selection in pediatric CKD prioritizes calcium load safety, avoiding aluminum binders, and balancing dietary phosphate control with protein preservation.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of phosphate binders in managing hyperphosphatemia in pediatric CKD.
Learn strategies to balance dietary phosphate control while preserving protein intake.
Identify safety considerations for calcium-based and non-calcium phosphate binders.
Monitor calcium, phosphate, and PTH trends to guide binder adjustments.
key_facts: Phosphate-lowering therapy should begin when phosphate is persistently elevated, alongside calcium and PTH trends.
Avoid long-term aluminum-containing binders due to toxicity risks.
Limit calcium-based binders in cases of hypercalcemia or suppressed PTH to prevent adynamic bone disease.
Non-calcium binders should be considered when total calcium exposure from diet and medications is high.
Binder efficacy requires administration with every phosphate-containing meal or snack.
Corrective steps before dietary phosphate restriction include binder timing, removing phosphate additives, and treating metabolic acidosis and constipation.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Start phosphate-lowering therapy when phosphate is progressively or persistently elevated, interpreted alongside calcium and PTH trends rather than a single value. (KDIGO 2017 Clinical Practice Guideline Update)
document_key_topics: Phosphate binder selection in pediatric CKD
Calcium load safety and hypercalcemia prevention
Monitoring trends in calcium, phosphate, and PTH
